description_long: This is a dark-colored, open-ended wrench with a robust, flat metal body and a wide, flared head. The wrench features two fixed jaws at the top, each with a slightly angled opening, suitable for gripping and turning nuts or bolts. The handle is straight, tapering slightly towards the head, and includes a circular hole near the base, likely for hanging or storage. The surface appears smooth and metallic, with a matte finish and no visible grip pattern or rubberized sections. There are no visible signs of rust, wear, or branding, giving it a clean and utilitarian appearance. This tool is typical for use in garages, workshops, or urban maintenance settings.
description: A dark, open-ended metal wrench with a wide head and smooth, flat handle, featuring a hole at the base for hanging. The surface is matte and unmarked, with fixed jaws for gripping nuts or bolts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, flared jaws and the full length of the handle with a visible hole at the base.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the thin, flat body and the angled orientation of the jaws.
description_view_2: Top-down view emphasizing the open-ended jaws and the symmetrical, flat handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the flatness and length of the handle and the open jaws.
